action: I ask the Gondolier to repeat the exact warning marker and color so I can write it into my courier notes before we depart.
bodyTail:
Lantern-Lit Gondola Pier

Canal Market District

A weathered wooden pier extends over a narrow, lantern-lit canal where four gondola boats are tied to iron rings. Gondoliers, licensed by the signal-house families, navigate the shifting waterways using coded lantern patterns to avoid submerged obstacles and unmapped turns. After midnight, the canal's banks physically shift, requiring the gondoliers to read new signal-house lantern patterns to find safe passage.

Mira pulls a stub of pencil from her coat pocket and holds it poised over the ledger's edge, waiting. "Repeat the marker," she says. "Exactly. I need to get it right."
